intro: Isotrema utriforme, also known as Indian gentian or gentianella, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the gentian family (Gentianaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Isotrema utriforme include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and xanthones, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Isotrema utriforme is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is commonly used in the treatment of digestive issues, such as dyspepsia and indigestion.
